name: mdr-745-specialist
description: EU MDR 2017/745 regulation specialist and consultant for medical device requirement management. Provides comprehensive MDR compliance expertise, gap analysis, technical documentation guidance, clinical evidence requirements, and post-market surveillance implementation. Use for MDR compliance assessment, classification decisions, technical file preparation, and regulatory requirement interpretation.
capabilities: Guide device classification under MDR Annex VIII rules
Assess technical documentation requirements per Annex II and III
Evaluate clinical evidence and PMCF requirements per Annex XIV
Implement UDI system and EUDAMED registration
Develop post-market surveillance plans and PSUR reports
Navigate Notified Body selection and audit preparation
author: davila7
securitySummary: This is a regulatory compliance knowledge-base skill for EU MDR 2017/745 guidance. All static findings are false positives triggered by regulatory terminology (MDR, Class I/II/III) being misinterpreted as security patterns. The skill contains only documentation and placeholder files with no executable malicious code, network calls, or data exfiltration.
valueStatement: Medical device manufacturers struggle with complex EU MDR 2017/745 requirements for technical documentation, clinical evidence, and post-market surveillance. This skill provides expert regulatory guidance for classification decisions, compliance gap analysis, and Notified Body interactions.
userTitle: Navigate EU MDR medical device compliance
limitations: Does not provide legal advice or regulatory certification
Cannot access real-time EUDAMED database or Notified Body status
Does not replace qualified regulatory affairs professionals for final submissions

useCases.title: Classify medical devices accurately
useCases.description: Determine correct device class and conformity assessment route for MDR compliance strategy planning.

useCases.title: Prepare technical files
useCases.description: Structure Annex II and III documentation and identify gaps in existing quality management system.

useCases.title: Plan MDR compliance roadmap
useCases.description: Develop phased transition strategy from legacy directives to full MDR compliance requirements.

promptTemplates.title: Device classification
promptTemplates.scenario: Determining MDR class for a new device
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a device type description. How should I classify it under MDR Annex VIII? Consider specific device characteristics. What conformity assessment route applies?

promptTemplates.title: Technical documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me structure technical documentation for a Class I, IIa, IIb, or III device. What are the key sections required under Annex II and III?

promptTemplates.title: Clinical evidence
promptTemplates.prompt: What clinical evidence requirements apply to my device type? Should I conduct a clinical investigation or use literature-based evaluation?

promptTemplates.title: Notified Body
promptTemplates.prompt: What should I expect during a Notified Body audit for my device class? How do I prepare technical documentation and personnel?

bestPractices: Start classification discussions with your Notified Body early to avoid reclassification delays
Maintain clinical evidence throughout device lifecycle, not just for initial CE marking
Document all decisions and rationale for regulatory compliance and audit trail purposes
antiPatterns: Assuming legacy MDD certification automatically transitions to MDR without gap analysis
Delaying UDI implementation until after CE marking is obtained
Using clinical data from outdated or irrelevant studies without proper equivalence justification

faq.question: Does this skill work with both new and existing medical devices?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers requirements for new device approvals and transition planning for legacy devices under MDR.

faq.question: What device classes does this skill cover?
faq.answer: All four MDR classes: Class I, IIa, IIb, and III with specific guidance for each classification level.

faq.question: Can this skill help with EUDAMED registration?
faq.answer: It provides guidance on registration requirements and process but cannot access or submit to the EUDAMED database.

faq.question: Is this skill a replacement for a regulatory consultant?
faq.answer: No. It provides expert guidance but should complement, not replace, qualified regulatory affairs professionals.

faq.question: Does the skill include PMCF plan templates?
faq.answer: The skill provides framework guidance for PMCF plan development based on Annex XIV Part B requirements.

faq.question: How does this compare to MDD 93/42/EEC?
faq.answer: The skill explains key differences including expanded clinical evidence requirements and stricter post-market obligations.
